search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng141 cellulitis reassessment worsening pain spread microbiology follow-up
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic infection diagnosis or treatment-failure conclusion
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Medicine names, doses, routes and durations were excluded.
Clinical diagnosis and response remain clinician-assessed.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults or children returning for review of a clinician-suspected or established bacterial skin infection; unusual exposure, periorbital, pregnancy, immune, vascular, diabetic, and pediatric contexts need qualified review.
setting_applicability: UAE primary, dermatology, pediatric, wound, infectious-disease, and hospital reassessment.
UAE_applicability: Reassessment fields are transferable, but UAE antimicrobial resistance, formulary, microbiology, emergency, facility, and referral pathways remain local.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG141 recommendations were reviewed on 2026-07-14; material recency limits remain.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact skin-infection reassessment section supports documenting interval history, change, symptoms, clinician skin inspection, distribution or extent, general appearance, secondary infection signs, severe pain, immune context and already available microbiology without asserting cellulitis, erysipelas, or treatment failure.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting rapid or significant worsening, lack of improvement, systemic illness, pain out of proportion, spread, underlying conditions, serious alternative diagnoses, and microbiology results during clinician reassessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact clinician-referral section supports documenting a clinician-entered plan, safety net, arranged follow-up and patient questions only; it does not generate escalation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting clinician-identified serious illness, periorbital location, unusual exposure, treatment non-response, lymphangitis, oral-intake limitation, and an already made referral or specialist discussion; it does not generate urgency.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Site, extent, exposure, broken-skin, skin-tone and previous-treatment context was reviewed for follow-up applicability.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting skin redness and swelling extent, site, severity, broken skin, penetrating or water exposure, microbiology already obtained, skin-tone visibility, and treatment already prescribed without generating diagnosis or treatment.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact infection type, site and dimensions, exposure, diagnosis, previous medicine and adherence, response, culture result interpretation, antibiotic, dose, route, duration, admission, referral, and review interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
